observations.recommendation:
1.8 We have recommended that the -

Designate conduct a yearly physical count of local road network
projects and accordingly update the local road map of the MLGU;
1.8.2 Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant – Designate
and Municipal General Services Officer – Designate the description
and cost segregation of road components of various road
infrastructure projects for their bases in recording in the Local
Road Network Ledger Card and maintenance of Local Road
Network Ledger Card, respectively;
1.8.3 Municipal General Services Officer – Designate maintain a Local
Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
1.8.4 Municipal Accountant – Designate keep and maintain Local Road
Network Ledger Card for roads and road components for every
road network and reconcile the same with the result of the physical
inventory taking at the end of the year. Also, provide for the
depreciation for the local road network and disclosure in the Notes
to the Financial Statements.

2.10 We have reiterated our recommendation that –
2.10.1 Management:
Conduct the complete annual physical count of PPE in
accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the
inventory taking for this purpose;
See to it that a copy of the RPCPPE shall be submitted to COA
for verification not later than January 31, of each year pursuant
to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS; for examination.
2.10.2 General Services Officer-Designate:
Maintain PPE Cards and ARE/PAR and ensure that the
ARE/PAR be renewed every after three years pursuant to
Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective
management and control over PPE;
2.10.3 General Services Officer-Designate and Municipal Accountant-
Designate reconcile their property records with the result of
physical count to ensure that PPE recorded in the property records
really exists and that all existing PPE are recorded in the books, in
order to establish correctness and completeness of balances of PPE
accounts. Any variance shall be dealt accordingly and
subsequently adjusted as necessary; and
2.10.4 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPE, if any.

3.11 We have recommended that the:
3.11.1 Municipal Accountant – Designate and OIC – Municipal Treasurer
reconcile their records every month to thresh out discrepancies and
adjust their records as necessary, to fairly present the balances;
3.11.2 OIC – Municipal Treasurer to submit the SEF Cashbook in order to
fully verify the entries and balances therein; and
3.11.3 Municipal Accountant – Designate, trace the difference and adjust
the books accordingly. Henceforth, ensure preparation of monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements and submission thereof to the audit
team every 10 days after the end of each month, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of PD No.
1445.

necessary adjusting journal entries for the various accounting
errors noted in Item 4.3 to correct the balance of the Cash Local
Treasury account;

accountable officer or the OIC - Municipal Treasurer then and
demand the for the immediate deposit of the ₱290,693.14
undeposited collections; and
4.8.3 Institute safeguard and control to make sure that the OIC -
Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections promptly and intact the
full amount to the authorized government deposit pursuant to
Section 69 (1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Section 32
of the Manual on NGAS to avoid possible misuse or loss occasioned
by fortuitous events to the disadvantage of the Municipality.

necessary adjusting journal entries for the various accounting
errors noted in Item 5.3 to correct the balance of the Advances for

settlement or liquidation of their respective outstanding cash
advances;

accumulation of unliquidated cash advances;
5.7.4 Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain subsidiary
ledgers to aide in tracking and monitoring the balance of cash
advances; and
5.7.5 Henceforth, adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation
of cash advances.

determine whether collections during the year are included in
the beginning balances, and effect necessary adjustments to
bring the correct balances of the accounts.

Treasurer coordinate and reconcile their records with the Municipal
Assessor to establish correctness of collectible real property taxes
and determine any changes/updates in the real properties.

Local Accountant, among others:
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network.
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
General Service Officer:
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components
Municipal Engineer:

Municipal Accountant-designate keep and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same with the result of the physical inventory taking at the end of the year, and make disclosure about road networks on the Notes to the Financial Statements.

11.3.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer submit the duly accomplished supporting
documents enumerated in Annex C for terminal leave benefits.
Henceforth, they should ensure that transactions are properly
supported with complete documentations before payment be made in
order to establish the validity of the claims.

11.4.1 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the
various payees to submit the lacking documents enumerated in Annex
D. Henceforth, ensure that all required supporting documents are
attached to the disbursement vouchers before these are processed and
approved for payment

11.5.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit all the
supporting documents required in the purchases of supplies and
medicines. Henceforth, procurement of supplies should be done in
accordance with the pertinent section of R.A. No. 9184 and
disbursement should be supported with complete document to
establish their validity, legality and correctness of entries made.

11.6.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures that all
expenditures for repairs and maintenance of equipment are fully
supported with the relevant documentations. Further, the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ascertain the completeness of the
attachments before any payment for repairs and maintenance of
equipment are made so as to assure the validity and legality of the
transaction.

12.5 We have recommended that management:
12.1 instruct the Municipal Treasurer/Accountant to immediately submit all
the necessary documents enumerated in Annex H to support the
payment of the development projects; and
12.2 strictly adhere with the policies and procedures on the documentary
requirements for all disbursements/purchases before payments are
made.

13.10 We have recommended the following:
13.10.1 that the Municipality submit the following documents:

- List of beneficiaries specifying therein the contact number,
description of the item received, quantity received, address of the
recipients;
- DTI Registration of the supplier
- Certificate of Transfer of Large Cattle
- Request for Quotation, Purchase Order, Purchase Requests and
Inspection Reports
- Official Receipt issued by the supplier, if applicable
- Acknowledgement receipt, if supplier is not issuing official
receipts

13.10.2 Justify the validity of their claims.
13.10.3 Henceforth, the Municipality should ensure that financial
transactions should conform with established rules and regulations
and to their prescribed usages.
13.10.4 Discontinue granting Municipal Community Assistance Program
under the name of SB member. Checks should be directly addressed
to the suppliers not with the municipal officials and employees.
13.10.5 Submit the official receipts

14.9 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and
henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different
rules and regulations cited above.

15.9 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should promptly deposit
all collections remitted or turned over to him by the collectors pursuant to the
above-mentioned provisions to avoid possible loss through theft or
misappropriation.

16.5 We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee conduct
procurements through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and
its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the
IRR. Alternative method of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly
exceptional cases provided in R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring
entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the municipality is
obtained.

17.2.8 We have recommended that Management:
17.2.8.1 enact a resolution, and open a separate depository account
for the 20% Development Fund and regularly transfer the
20% Internal Revenue Allotment from the General Fund
account to this account pursuant to Sec. 313 of RA 7160.
17.2.8.2 maintain a special account and a complete set of subsidiary
ledgers under the General Fund to easily monitor fund
utilization and status thereof and to enable the generation of
financial statements at any given time.

18.2.7 We have recommended that management:
18.2.7.1 prepare and submit to the Audit Team the
accomplishment/utilization report for the 20%
Development Fund to properly identify and trace the
expenditures that were charged against the fund.
18.2.7.2 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that complete
documentary requirements are attached prescribed by
COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing of disbursements.

18.3.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator and other concerned agency personnel meticulously plan, review,
integrate and implement in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan
programs, projects and activities that yield permanent benefit/capital in
nature and in accordance with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2017-1 to attain the purpose of the fund.

19.3 We have recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Officer prepare and submit regularly the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF in accordance with the format provided under COA
Circular No. 2012-002, to facilitate timely review of the transactions of the fund.

20.7 We have recommended that:
20.7.1 the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal
Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate Special Trust
Fund for the unexpended balance of the LDRRM;
20.7.2 the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund
to be opened;
20.7.3 the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account and a complete
set of subsidiary for the said fund.

21.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint/hire an officer to
handle the responsibilities of a Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management for
a more effective and efficient implementation and monitoring of disaster
related activities.

22.4 We have recommended that the Municipality observe the scheduled GAD
Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No.
2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 to
facilitate the timely submission to the PPDO and the subsequent review and
endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

23.5 We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System -
23.5.1 Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for
gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;
23.5.2 Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender
issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are
responsive to the said issues; and
23.5.3 Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01,
which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.

24.5 We have recommended that:
24.5.1 The Municipality ensure that GAD programs, projects, and activities
are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; and
24.5.2 The GFPS review its functions, duties and roles set forth per PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and strengthen its monitoring
and evaluation to successfully address gender issues and concerns as
well as to provide accurate reports on the implementation.

25.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer deduct, withhold and remit the corresponding internal revenue
taxes as mandated by laws, rules and regulations on all its future purchases of
goods and services to help generate additional income for the government and
to avoid possible imposition of sanctions by the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
We again reiterated the following recommendation:
• That the Municipal Treasurer should be properly bonded as provided in Annex C of Treasury Circular 02-2009 and should be granted cash advance only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating expenses.
• The Municipal Mayor and all elective officials shall only be entitled to claim travelling expenses for official travels.
• The Municipal Accountant should support all entries made in the General Journals for the liquidation of cash advance with complete documentation
• The Municipal Accountant should also submit the liquidation reports and supporting documents for the cash advance of the Municipal Mayor and other elective officials.
• Municipal Community Assistance Program should be directly paid to suppliers and not as cash advance to the Sanggunian Bayan members
• The Municipal Treasurer should make an estimate of all the expenses for her cash advance before withdrawal and should be granted only cash advances for payment of salaries, wages, honorarium and petty operating expenses.
• All disbursements exceeding ₱15,000.00 must be made by check direct to the payee.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to enforce collection of the livelihood loans. For Municipal Employees, the amount should be deducted from their salaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR Reiterated in this 2015 report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management justify the incurrence of overdraft at year-end, determine the cause of overdraft and refund the amount so as to have sufficient cash balance for payment of liabilities. Henceforth, monitor all disbursements to guarantee that cash overdraft is avoided.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the submission of the required documents to avoid audit disallowances. Henceforth, she should ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before these are processed and approved for payment in accordance with EO 298 and NGAS Manual.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly remit mandatory obligations as prescribed by laws, rules and regulations so that the municipal employees could conveniently avail of the services and benefits offered by the agencies, and interests and penalties will be avoided. Refund of penalty must be imposed to the persons responsible for late payments.
We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should promptly deposit all collections remitted or turned over to him by the collectors to avoid loss through theft or misappropriation.

We have recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period of ten (10) days from the beginning of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should look into the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting Office to facilitate submission of accounts.

We have recommended that management submit justification for the failure to enact a Supplemental Budget to authorize the disbursement of the 2015 RA 7171 fund releases. Also, we have also recommended that henceforth, a Supplemental Budget, together with a Sanggunian Bayan Resolution be prepared and approved before shares in tobacco excise tax are disbursed.

We have recommended that the Bids and Award Committee should abide by the rule that all procurements should be done through public bidding. Alternative mode of procurements could only be resorted to in cases prescribed in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We also have recommended that copies of contracts and purchase orders are submitted to the office of the auditor five (5) days after perfection for the prompt review and evaluation of the reasonableness of the cost.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit all the supporting documents required in the implementation of infrastructure projects in accordance with the pertinent sections of Revised IRR of RA 9184 to establish their validity, legality and correctness of entries made.

We have recommended the following:
• The Municipality shall maintain a special account for the 20% Development Fund in compliance with Section 313 of the Local Government Code to facilitate monitoring of development projects. Operations of the Fund should be accounted for through the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of financial statements at any given time. Moreover, regularly transfer the 20% development fund to its account from the General Fund account to reflect accurate financial information relative to the fund as a special account.
• The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator integrate in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan programs/projects that yield permanent benefit/capital in nature and in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1 to attain the purpose of the fund.
• Disbursement vouchers and the required documents shall also be submitted to establish the legality and

1.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate trace the period
or date of acquisition of the buildings and compute the depreciation based on the
estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007. Prior

2.7 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing
disbursement vouchers. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly
reports and journals are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid
vouchers, payrolls and official receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section
347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 2009.

3.11 In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that:
 Management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete
annual physical count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on
the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be invited to
witness the inventory taking for this purpose;
 the Property and Accounting Units maintain complete and accurate PPELC for
each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 on the NGAS Manual for LGUs
in order to promote effective monitoring and controls over PPE;
 a reconciliation of the Property and Accounting records be done after the
conduct of physical count;
 the Inventory Committee submit the Physical Inventory Reports to the
Property Officer and COA Office for verification and reconciliation of the
result of the physical inventory with the complete Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards and the Property Cards maintained by the Property
and Accounting Units;
 ARE/PAR be maintained and renewed every three years in accordance with the
Instructions on the use of ARE/PAR;
 the Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed procedures in
COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable PPE, if any; and
 Management transfer to the Department of Education the cost of the school
building through a Journal Entry Voucher to be drawn by the Municipal
Accountant and copy furnished the Accountant of DepEd.

4.4 We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the
bases in the preparation of the financial statements.

5.7 We have recommended that:
 Management promptly conduct an inventory of its road network system and the
GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
 The Municipal Accountant trace the recorded road, highways and bridges which
were closed to government equity in the prior years and subsequently record in
its books of accounts the amount of road works equivalent to the required
transition provision.

6.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort to identify
the sources of the unreconciled difference between the Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds through reconciliation of the accounts and effect immediately
the adjustments upon verification.

7.6 We again reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant submit all
the supporting documents to prove that the project has been completed, request for
the inspection of the project and secure credit notice to release the Municipality
from accountability to the Department of Agriculture.

8.4 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to enforce collection of the livelihood
loans. For Municipal Employees, the amount should be deducted from their
salaries.

9.1.4 We reiterated our recommendation to the management that cash advances be
supported with estimate/breakdown of expenses to be paid, in accordance
with Section 89 of PD 1445 and Annex B of COA Circular 2012-03. Also, we
reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer should be
granted cash advances only for salaries, wages, honorarium and petty
operating expenses.

9.2.6 We have recommended that payments above ₱15,000.00 shall be made
through the issuance of checks in accordance with the above pertinent rules and regulation
and that the same shall be paid directly to the payee. This would lessen the cash
advances of the Municipal Treasurer and would facilitate verification of legality
and correctness of her accountability.

11.11 We have recommended that management:
 Instruct the concerned offices to submit necessary documents to prove the
validity of claims for meals and snacks in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD
1445 and COA Circular 2012-001;
 Instruct the department heads to make purchases to registered suppliers and
that payments made to them should be subjected to applicable withholding
taxes in compliance with existing revenue regulations; and
 Procurements exceeding the thresholds for shopping and/or small value
procurements shall be done through public bidding in compliance with RA
9184.

12.4 We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive hire a qualified Municipal
Accountant who shall take charge of both the accounting and internal audit
services of the agency to improve the internal control system, financial reporting
and operations of the Municipality.

13.6 In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
 That the Municipal Budget Officer be instructed to prepare and strictly comply
with the requirements of properly accomplishing the Obligation Requests for
all expenditures. Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations shall
be maintained to properly monitor the budget of the agency and serve as the
basis to prepare the Statement of Allotment, Appropriations, Obligations and
Balances; and
 The Municipal Accountant be instructed not to process claims without the
required certification as to the availability of appropriation including the
specific cost centers or function/program/project where expenses will be
charged.

14.8 We have recommended that the management instruct the Municipal
Treasurer/Accountant to immediately submit all the necessary documents to
support the payment of development projects. Henceforth, strictly adhere with
the policies and procedures on the documentary requirements for all
disbursements/purchases before payments are made.

15.6 We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses on procurement
of goods. Hence, all procurements of the municipality above ₱100,000.00 shall be
done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless
justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the IRR and as
recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity.
Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional
cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184. In all instances, the procuring entity shall
ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

16.7 We have recommended that the members of Bids and Awards Committee should
collect honorarium only when there’s a collection from sale of bid documents or
savings as approved by the Sangguniang Bayan. The Municipal Accountant
should not be designated as BAC member.

16.8 We also have recommended that the honoraria should be refunded by the
employees concerned.

17.6 We have recommended the following:
 That the Sanggunian Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor
and the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF;
 That the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund to be
opened; and
 That the Municipal Accountant maintains a special account and a complete set
of subsidiary for the said fund.

17.11 In view of the observations, we have recommended the following:
 Submit list of beneficiaries with their addresses, quantity received and
signatures to support the disbursements; and
 The Municipal Officials should ensure that the financial assistance, whether in
cash or in kind should be personally received by the intended beneficiaries to
safeguard the resources of the government and are managed and utilized in
conformance to established rules and regulations and with the prescribed
usages.

17.17 We have recommended that management should have utilized ₱1,548,766.28
(70% x ₱2,212,523.26) for procuring rescue and response equipment that can be
used during calamity in order to prepare the municipality to cope with
calamity/disasters and lessen or mitigate its adverse effects.

17.23 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint personnel to handle
the responsibilities of a Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and
instruct him to prepare and submit an LDRRMFIP. He should instruct the
Municipal Accountant to submit a monthly utilization report on DDRMF and
disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the unexpended balance as at year-
end. Management should familiarize themselves on rules and regulations
pertaining disaster risk reduction management.

18.2.2 We have recommended that the Municipality shall maintain a special
account for the 20% Development Fund in compliance with Section 313 of
the Local Government Code to facilitate monitoring of development
projects. Operations of the Fund should be accounted for through the
maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to enable the generation of
financial statements at any given time. Moreover, regularly transfer the
20% development fund to its account from the General Fund account to
reflect accurate financial information relative to the fund as a special
account.

18.3.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator integrate in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan
programs/projects that yield permanent benefit/capital in nature and in
accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1 to attain the purpose of
the fund.

18.4.5 We have recommended that accomplishment report and schedule of
disbursements should be submitted to properly identify and trace the
expenditures that were charged against 20% development fund. The
Municipal Accountant should also be guided by the revised documentary
requirements as per COA Circular 2012-001 in the processing of
disbursements.

19.6 We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint a GAD Focal Point who
will prepare accomplishment report and monitor the proper implementation of
the GAD Budget. We also recommend that at least 5% of the budget be allocated
for GAD.
